title: National Underwear Day Gets Undie-niably Fun!
story: Every August 5, people celebrate National Underwear Day by showing off their most colorful, silly, or comfortable undies! Some folks post photos of their favorite underwear patterns on social media, while others wear bright boxers or fun panties as outerwear. Stores offer special sales on underwear, and people share jokes about their most embarrassing underwear moments. It's a lighthearted day that reminds everyone that even the clothes we can't see deserve some attention and fun!
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate National Underwear Day?
activity_choices: By posting photos of colorful underwear on social media
By wearing only socks all day
By throwing underwear in the trash
activity_answer: By posting photos of colorful underwear on social media
follow_up_question: What's the silliest underwear pattern you've ever seen?

title: Work Like a Dog Day Gets Tails Wagging
story: Dogs work harder than you might think! On August 5, National Work Like a Dog Day celebrates both hardworking people and amazing working dogs. People share stories about their busy jobs, while others post pictures of police dogs, guide dogs, and farm dogs doing important work. It's a day to appreciate everyone who works their tails off!
activity_question: What types of working dogs do people celebrate on this holiday?
activity_choices: Police dogs, guide dogs, and farm dogs
Only pet dogs that do tricks
activity_answer: Police dogs, guide dogs, and farm dogs
follow_up_question: What job would you want a dog to help you with?

title: Sweet Seeds and Summer Treats
story: Every August 5, people across America celebrate National Watermelon Day by chomping into the juiciest fruit of summer! Families gather for watermelon-eating contests, seeing who can finish their slice fastest without using their hands. Kids love spitting the black seeds as far as they can, while adults carve watermelons into fancy fruit bowls shaped like baskets or even animals. Grocery stores offer special deals on different varieties, from tiny personal watermelons to giant ones that weigh over 20 pounds. Social media fills with photos of people enjoying pink and red slices at picnics, barbecues, and pool parties, making this the perfect day to beat the summer heat with nature's sweetest treat.
activity_question: What do kids love to do with watermelon seeds during National Watermelon Day celebrations?
activity_choices: Spit them as far as they can
Count how many are in each slice
Use them as tiny musical instruments
activity_answer: Spit them as far as they can
bonus_fact: The heaviest watermelon ever grown weighed 350 pounds - that's heavier than a baby elephant!

title: Stop, Look, and Celebrate Traffic Lights!
story: Red means stop, green means go, and August 5 means it's time to celebrate traffic lights! National Traffic Light Day honors those colorful signals that keep our streets safe and organized. People mark this day by sharing photos of interesting traffic lights they spot, learning about road safety, and appreciating how these simple devices prevent chaos at every intersection. Some communities even host safety events where kids practice crossing streets properly. Next time you're waiting at a red light, remember that these helpful signals have been guiding traffic for over a century!
activity_question: What do people do to celebrate National Traffic Light Day?
activity_choices: Share photos of traffic lights they find
Paint their cars red, yellow, and green
Drive through red lights for fun
Turn off all traffic lights for the day
activity_answer: Share photos of traffic lights they find
bonus_fact: The first electric traffic light was installed in Cleveland, Ohio in 1914!
